name: IBS Symptom Severity Scale
description: A validated instrument for measuring the severity of irritable bowel syndrome symptoms. Assesses abdominal pain, distension, bowel satisfaction, and quality of life impact over the past 10 days.
instructions: Answer all questions based on how you currently feel (over the last 10 days). For visual analog scale questions, place your mark anywhere on the line between 0-100% to indicate severity as accurately as possible.

questions.question: How severe is your abdominal (belly) pain?
How many days do you typically experience abdominal pain every 10 days?
questions.note: Value is multiplied by 10 to give 0-100 score component

questions.question: How severe is your abdominal distension (bloating, swollen or tight tummy)?
questions.note: Women should ignore distension related to periods

questions.question: How satisfied are you with your bowel habits?
questions.note: Scale is reversed: 0 = very satisfied, 100 = not at all satisfied

questions.question: How much does your Irritable Bowel Syndrome affect or interfere with your life in general?

severityBands.label: Remission / Control
severityBands.description: IBS symptoms in remission or well controlled.

severityBands.label: Severe IBS
severityBands.description: Severe IBS symptom severity requiring clinical attention.

interpretation.scoringMethod: Sum of 5 components: (1) Pain severity (0-100), (2) Days with pain × 10 (0-100), (3) Distension severity (0-100), (4) Bowel dissatisfaction (0-100), (5) Life interference (0-100). Total range: 0-500.
interpretation.clinicalSignificance: A 50-point reduction is generally considered clinically meaningful improvement.

references.citation: Francis CY, Morris J, Whorwell PJ. The irritable bowel severity scoring system: a simple method of monitoring irritable bowel syndrome and its progress. Aliment Pharmacol Ther. 1997;11(2):395-402.

metadata.author: Francis CY, Morris J, Whorwell PJ
